fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 46-year-old White male with schizophrenia presenting with severe treatment-resistant symptoms.
fields.Subjective: Mr. Anderson attended appointment disheveled and appeared severely distressed. Ex-wife present confirms he takes clozapine 450 mg nightly via her supervision during weekly visits, adherence confirmed by recent clozapine blood level. Despite therapeutic clozapine level, continues experiencing severe unremitting symptoms. Reports continuous auditory hallucinations of multiple voices throughout entire day commanding him to harm himself and others. Endorses complex paranoid delusions that FBI monitors him through implanted devices and neighbors are part of surveillance network. Sleep severely disrupted at 2-4 hours nightly due to voices and hypervigilance. Appetite very poor, eating one meal daily, weight down to 142 lbs (lost 18 lbs over four months). Unable to work for past three years, on disability. Lives alone in studio apartment, ex-wife checks on him weekly. No social contacts. Spends days pacing and checking for surveillance equipment. Mood 'terrible and scared' rated 2/10. Denies substance use. Ex-wife very concerned about deterioration. Safety: Endorses frequent passive SI, denies current intent or plan, denies HI despite command hallucinations. (Evaluated by Dr. Steven Harrison, MD, Psychiatrist)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Middle-aged male, disheveled, poor hygiene, appears frightened and agitated
Mental_Status_Examination.Speech: Rapid, pressured, disorganized with frequent derailment
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Severe persistent command auditory hallucinations with violent content, complex paranoid delusions about surveillance and persecution
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Process: Severely disorganized, tangential, dominated by psychotic themes

Behavioral_Health_Checkup.C_SSRS: Frequent passive suicidal ideation without current intent or plan

Laboratory_Data.Recent_Lab_Results: Clozapine level 402 ng/mL (therapeutic); ANC 3800 (acceptable); glucose 124 mg/dL; significant weight loss documented.

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Treatment-resistant schizophrenia with severe persistent symptoms despite therapeutic clozapine levels.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives alone; unable to work, on disability; isolated with only ex-wife contact; severe functional impairment; safety concerns.
Assessment_and_Plan: Adherence confirmed through supervision and therapeutic drug levels.
Severe treatment-resistant symptoms despite adequate clozapine trial.
Command hallucinations and weight loss very concerning.
Discussed hospitalization for safety and treatment augmentation.
Patient refuses inpatient admission at this time.
Will augment with antipsychotic - adding risperidone 2 mg BID.
Increased monitoring - ex-wife to check in daily.
fields.Author: Note written by Dr. Steven Harrison, MD, Psychiatrist — Community Mental Health Crisis Services, 5521 Crisis Center Blvd, Seattle, WA 98101, Phone: (555) 447-8836, Fax: (555) 447-8837
